Risk Description: Because of delays in completing Pre-Development deliverables (User Stories, UX Design, Functional Requirements, HLD approvals) across multiple releases (0.2-0.8) as evidenced in both WSR status report and JIRA tracking system, there is a risk that development start dates will be pushed back, which could lead to cascading delays in UAT timelines and overall Release 1 delivery by mid-July/August 2026.
Potential Impact: Cascading delays to UAT and Release 1 go-live; potential SLA breach; increased project costs due to extended timelines; resource reallocation challenges.

Risk Description: Because of pending AWS Bedrock setup, ADO to AWS pipeline integration, and FMS configuration for Billie Part 2 (AI invoice processing using Claude Sonnet 4.5) as confirmed in WSR infrastructure section and JIRA dependency tracking, there is a risk that development and QA completion will be delayed by at least 4 weeks after infrastructure is ready, which could lead to UAT start date postponement and impact Release 1 delivery.
Potential Impact: Delayed UAT start; Release 1 go-live at risk; AI-driven invoice processing unavailable for beta customers; potential cost overruns due to infrastructure delays.

SLA Metric: Data gateway and workspace setup pending
Risk Description: Because of incomplete Power BI infrastructure setup (data gateway, workspace configuration) and pending functional requirements for standard reports as documented in WSR Reporting section and JIRA infrastructure issues, there is a risk that development end dates cannot be finalized and reporting capabilities will not be ready for UAT, which could lead to incomplete business intelligence functionality in Release 1.
Tech Stack: Power BI, Data Gateway, BI Tools
Potential Impact: Reporting functionality unavailable for Release 1; business intelligence gaps; delayed decision-making capabilities; potential compliance and audit risks.

Risk Description: Because of pending approvals for Functional Requirements (7/31), Design (8/8), Data Models (8/1), and HLD for Payments/Disbursements module as tracked in both WSR dependencies and JIRA approval workflow, there is a risk that development start date will be significantly delayed beyond the planned July 15 start, which could lead to rework, scope compression, and quality compromises.
Potential Impact: Development rework; compressed testing timelines; potential quality issues; Release 1 timeline at risk.

Risk Description: Because of incomplete user research and pending approvals for Functional Requirements (8/1), Design (8/1), Data Models, and HLD for the Vendors module as documented in WSR and JIRA, there is a risk that the revised development start date of August 4 will result in compressed development and testing cycles, which could lead to defects and incomplete functionality.
Potential Impact: Compressed development cycle; increased defect risk; potential functionality gaps; UAT quality concerns.

Risk Description: Because of pending HLD approvals for Approval Engine (Kannan, ETA 8/1) and Approval UI HLD (Keith, ETA 7/30), and development already started with HLD 7.2 as noted in WSR technical debt section and JIRA spike items, there is a risk of significant rework and architectural misalignment, which could lead to defects, integration issues, and timeline delays.
Potential Impact: Architectural rework; integration challenges; increased defect density; potential security and compliance gaps in approval workflows.

Risk Description: Because of 116 QA defects identified across sprints 15-20 and a defect leakage rate of approximately 4.8% (approaching the 5% threshold) as reported in WSR quality metrics and JIRA defect tracking, there is a risk that escaped defects will reach UAT and production, which could lead to customer-reported issues, rework, and potential SLA breaches.
Potential Impact: Customer-reported defects; UAT delays; production issues; potential SLA breach; increased support costs; reputational risk.

Risk Description: Because UAT for Release 0.4+0.8 was paused and a complete UAT readiness assessment from 0.1 through 0.4 is required before re-release to Axos as documented in WSR executive summary and JIRA UAT tracking, there is a risk that accumulated technical debt, incomplete regression testing, and unresolved defects will surface during UAT, which could lead to extended UAT cycles and delayed go-live.
Potential Impact: Extended UAT cycles; delayed go-live; increased defect discovery in UAT; potential scope reduction; customer dissatisfaction.

Risk Description: Because of recurring environment downtimes totaling 181 hours of team productivity loss since May 14, 2026 as tracked in WSR issues section and JIRA infrastructure incidents, there is a risk that development, testing, and integration activities will continue to be disrupted, which could lead to further schedule delays, reduced code quality, and team morale issues.
Potential Impact: Continued productivity loss; schedule delays; reduced code quality; team morale issues; increased project costs.

Risk Title: HLD Approval Delay and Rework Risk
Risk Description: Because HLD completion and approval for Release 0.2 modules was delayed until July 29-31, and development was already complete as noted in WSR module readiness and JIRA technical spikes, there is a risk that architectural misalignments will require significant rework, which could lead to defects, integration issues, and schedule impacts for subsequent releases.
Potential Impact: Architectural rework; integration challenges; potential defects; schedule impacts for future releases; increased technical debt.

Risk Description: Because of the planned UI revamp using Play+ framework requiring installation in Axos environment (Phase 1: 7/23-8/7) and updating existing screens (Phase 2: 8/11-9/8) as outlined in WSR and JIRA UI tasks, there is a risk that UI inconsistencies, integration issues, and user experience gaps will emerge, which could lead to UAT delays and user adoption challenges.
Potential Impact: UI inconsistencies; integration issues; UAT delays; user adoption challenges; potential rework of existing screens.

Financial Metric: Fixed price: Schedule B; Hourly rate: $60-120/hr
Risk Description: Because Phase 2 operates under a fixed-price model with change management requiring either fixed-price quotes, hourly rates ($60-120/hr), or use of 300 Accrued Hours (25 hrs/month) as documented in contract terms, there is a risk that scope changes, requirement additions, and rework will exceed available Accrued Hours and require additional budget approvals, which could lead to cost overruns and contract disputes.
Potential Impact: Budget overruns; contract disputes; scope compression; potential descoping of features; stakeholder dissatisfaction.

Risk Description: Because Phase 2 scope includes integration with third-party payroll provider Rollfi (19 UI/UX screens, 59 APIs) and functional parity with Current Zenith System is required as specified in scope documents, there is a risk that Rollfi API limitations, integration delays, or lack of cooperation will prevent achieving functional parity, which could lead to scope gaps and customer dissatisfaction.
Potential Impact: Functional parity gaps; integration delays; scope reduction; customer dissatisfaction; potential need for alternative payroll provider.

Risk Description: Because Axos Bank will primarily handle integration of banking products and services with Rebuilt Zenith System, and Ascendion must verify functional parity with Current Zenith System as outlined in integration requirements, there is a risk that integration gaps will be identified late in the development cycle, which could lead to rework, schedule delays, and use of Accrued Hours for gap resolution.
Potential Impact: Late-stage integration gaps; rework; schedule delays; consumption of Accrued Hours; potential functional parity issues.

SLA Metric: Multiple features deferred from R1 to R2
Risk Description: Because multiple features have been deferred from Release 1 to Release 2 (including International Wires, ACH Confirmations, Credit Card Auto-Allocation, Dashboard Widgets, Task Management, Tickler System, Deposit Scheduling, Audit Log UI, File Management UI) as documented in WSR scope decisions and JIRA backlog, there is a risk that Release 1 will not meet minimum viable product requirements for beta customers, which could lead to delayed customer onboarding and reduced business value.
Potential Impact: Reduced Release 1 business value; delayed customer onboarding; potential customer dissatisfaction; competitive disadvantage.

Risk Title: Code Coverage and Quality Gap Risk
Risk Description: Because unit test coverage improved from 60% to 70% with AAVA generating 90% of unit test cases, but 30% of code remains untested as reported in WSR QA status and JIRA quality metrics, there is a risk that untested code paths will contain defects that escape to production, which could lead to production incidents, customer impact, and increased support costs.
Potential Impact: Production defects in untested code; customer impact; increased support costs; potential SLA breaches.

Risk Title: Performance Testing and SLA Compliance Risk
Risk Description: Because performance requirements are stringent (UI page rendering <=10 seconds, API response <=3 seconds) and performance testing strategy discussions are ongoing as noted in WSR performance section and JIRA testing tasks, there is a risk that performance testing will be inadequate or delayed, which could lead to failure to meet performance SLAs and require code refactoring or hardware capacity enhancements.
Potential Impact: Performance SLA breaches; code refactoring required; hardware capacity upgrades; delayed go-live; customer dissatisfaction.

Risk Title: Data Migration Strategy and Execution Risk
Risk Description: Because data migration strategy and estimates are not yet finalized, and the ability to have 5 beta customers ready at go-live depends on successful data migration from Legacy Zenith to NexLedgr as highlighted in WSR risks section and JIRA migration tasks, there is a risk that data migration delays or quality issues will prevent customer onboarding, which could lead to delayed go-live and manual data entry as a fallback.
Potential Impact: Delayed customer onboarding; go-live delays; data quality issues; potential manual data entry; increased project costs.

SLA Metric: DNS changes implemented; UI issues pending
Risk Description: Because AAVA 2.0 installation in Axos environment required DNS changes (completed) but has open UI issues (Execute tab, Agent Edit screen, KB creation visibility), and AKS cluster upgrade to 1.33 is pending (ETA 3/31) as documented in WSR tooling section, there is a risk that AAVA 2.0 adoption will be delayed or incomplete, which could lead to reduced productivity gains and inability to leverage agentic AI capabilities.
Potential Impact: Reduced productivity gains; inability to leverage AI capabilities; potential reversion to AAVA 1.0; schedule impacts.

Risk Description: Because regression automation coverage is at 80% across sprints 15-20, leaving 20% of regression testing manual as reported in WSR automation status and JIRA test metrics, there is a risk that manual regression testing will be time-consuming, error-prone, and insufficient to catch all regressions, which could lead to defect leakage and UAT delays.
Potential Impact: Manual regression testing delays; defect leakage; UAT delays; increased QA costs; potential production issues.

Risk Description: Because public and private API categorization is in progress and requires comprehensive documentation of background processes and API endpoints (ETA 3/9), and all private APIs must enforce service account token authentication as outlined in WSR security section and JIRA security tasks, there is a risk that incomplete or incorrect API categorization will create security vulnerabilities and authorization gaps, which could lead to compliance violations and security breaches.
Potential Impact: Security vulnerabilities; authorization gaps; compliance violations; potential security breaches; reputational damage.

Risk Description: Because BurpSuite security scan results have been extracted but not yet acted upon as noted in WSR tech debt and JIRA security bugs, there is a risk that identified security vulnerabilities will remain unresolved, which could lead to security breaches, compliance violations, and delayed production deployment.
Potential Impact: Unresolved security vulnerabilities; compliance violations; security breaches; delayed production deployment; audit failures.

Risk Title: Data Model Instability and Rework Risk
Risk Description: Because database models for Deposits/Receipts/Commissions went through multiple changes resulting in rework, and shared services component dependencies exist as documented in WSR milestone status and JIRA data model tasks, there is a risk that ongoing data model instability will continue to cause rework and integration issues, which could lead to defects, schedule delays, and quality concerns.
Potential Impact: Continued rework; integration issues; schedule delays; defects; quality concerns; increased technical debt.

SLA Metric: ~22.19 bugs per KLOC; Target: 2 bugs per KLOC
Risk Description: Because actual defect density is approximately 22.19 bugs per KLOC (thousand lines of code) compared to the target of 2 bugs per KLOC, exceeding the target by over 10x as reported in WSR quality metrics and JIRA defect analysis, there is a risk that code quality issues are systemic, which could lead to increased maintenance costs, production defects, and customer dissatisfaction.
Potential Impact: Systemic code quality issues; increased maintenance costs; production defects; customer dissatisfaction; technical debt accumulation.

Risk Description: Because cross-POD dependencies were not fully identified upfront, leading to coordination challenges across Alpha, Cypher, Nexus, and Apex teams as noted in WSR open items and JIRA coordination tasks, there is a risk that integration issues, conflicting changes, and dependency delays will continue to impact delivery, which could lead to schedule delays and quality issues.
Potential Impact: Integration issues; conflicting changes; dependency delays; schedule impacts; quality issues; team coordination challenges.

SLA Metric: Historical loss of key resources noted
Risk Title: Resource Attrition and Knowledge Loss Risk
Risk Description: Because historical project data shows loss of key team members (DB Architects, Solution Architect, Product Owner, Delivery Leader, Testing Lead) over 12 months as documented in historical risk patterns, there is a risk that key personnel attrition will occur during Phase 2, which could lead to knowledge loss, productivity decline, and schedule delays.
Potential Impact: Knowledge loss; productivity decline; schedule delays; increased onboarding costs; quality impacts.

Risk Description: Because token-based authentication has an accepted vulnerability where tokens remain valid until short expiration time even after logout, and this is an accepted risk across multiple Axos websites as documented in WSR security decisions, there is a risk that compromised tokens could be exploited before expiration, which could lead to unauthorized access and security incidents.
Potential Impact: Potential unauthorized access; security incidents; compliance concerns; limited window of vulnerability.

Risk Title: Software Version and Technical Debt Risk
Risk Description: Because Axos follows an N-1 versioning strategy for all software (currently Angular 18, .NET 8, SQL Server 2019, HTTP 1.1) and upgrades only when necessary as outlined in WSR tech decisions, there is a risk that delayed upgrades will create technical debt, security vulnerabilities, and compatibility issues, which could lead to increased maintenance costs and security risks.
Potential Impact: Technical debt accumulation; security vulnerabilities; compatibility issues; increased maintenance costs; potential upgrade challenges.

SLA Metric: Zero critical/high defects in R0.2 regression
Risk Description: Because Release 0.2 achieved zero critical or high defects in Ascendion regression testing, setting a high-quality bar as reported in WSR quality assessment, there is a risk that subsequent releases may not maintain this quality level due to increased complexity and compressed timelines, which could lead to quality degradation and defect leakage.
Potential Impact: Quality degradation in future releases; defect leakage; UAT issues; potential production defects; customer dissatisfaction.

Risk Description: Because standard reports requirements and reporting center requirements are pending Axos review and feedback on balance sheet (functional requirements and UX/UI update ETA 8/22) as documented in WSR reporting section and JIRA requirements tasks, there is a risk that late requirement finalization will compress development and testing cycles for reporting functionality, which could lead to incomplete or defective reporting capabilities in Release 1.
Potential Impact: Compressed development cycles; incomplete reporting functionality; defective reports; Release 1 gaps; business intelligence limitations.

Risk Description: Because there are 51 open UAT bugs as of 03/17 (12 High priority, 39 Medium priority) with 24 bugs in progress and 6 bugs not reproducible/sporadic as tracked in WSR UAT assessment and JIRA defect register, there is a risk that UAT bug resolution will delay UAT completion and go-live readiness, which could lead to extended UAT cycles and delayed Release 1 delivery.
Potential Impact: Extended UAT cycles; delayed go-live; potential scope reduction; customer dissatisfaction; increased support costs.

Risk Description: Because GoAnywhere file scanning enablement for Document Vault has file movement issues from Unscanned to Scanned folder as noted in WSR open items and troubleshooting meeting scheduled for 3/18, there is a risk that document processing functionality will be incomplete or unreliable, which could lead to UAT delays and operational issues with document management.
Potential Impact: Document processing delays; UAT delays; operational issues; potential data loss; compliance concerns.

Risk Description: Because auto-population of USPS address requires integration with third-party services and AXOS is currently evaluating what solutions are being used in other areas within AXOS with decision pending as highlighted in WSR executive attention section, there is a risk that delayed decision on approach will impact Milestone 1 delivery timelines, which could lead to scope gaps or workaround implementations.
Potential Impact: Milestone 1 delivery delays; scope gaps; potential workaround implementations; customer dissatisfaction.

Risk Title: Because Banking Integration DEV Cursory Testing for NACHA is in progress with data flowing and JE created but not posted, requiring ASC investigation as noted in WSR banking integration status, there is a risk that NACHA integration issues will delay banking functionality completion, which could lead to incomplete banking capabilities in Release 1.
Risk Description: Technical
Likelihood: Banking functionality delays; integration issues; Release 1 gaps; potential SLA impacts.

Risk Description: Because API regression automation is 56% completed with target of 100% by 04/09, and 5% cannot be automated as reported in WSR API automation status, there is a risk that incomplete API regression automation will result in manual testing gaps and delayed defect detection, which could lead to API defects escaping to production.
Potential Impact: Manual testing gaps; delayed defect detection; API defects in production; increased QA effort; potential SLA breaches.
